meaning: n.椹箘閾乿t.瑁呰箘閾佷簬
examples: In order to withstand tidal currents, juvenile horseshoe crabs frequently burrow in the sand.
Surprisingly, in areas where tidal currents are very weak, juvenile horseshoe crabs are found not to have significant barnacle populations, even though they seldom burrow.
After getting to town, the father got some cherries by selling the horseshoe.
Only mid-Devon falls outside a horseshoe of largely rural authorities extending south and east from the Fens.
Approached along a pitted dirt track, the rocky cove curves like a horseshoe around the lucent blue waters.
